channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Rain and cooler weather help South Korean fire crews battle devastating wildfires
item.description: Rain and cooler temperatures are helping South Korean fire crews as they battle the country’s worst-ever wildfires as the governor of the hardest-hit region called for overhauling response strategies to respond to the climate crisis that he says worsened the disaster.
item.dc:creator: Hyung-Jin Kim And Kim Tong-Hyung, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Lithuanian, Polish troops aid in 'search and recovery' for 4 missing US soldiers from Fort Stewart
item.description: A Lithuanian official told Fox News Digital about ongoing search efforts to recover four missing U.S. Army soldiers in coordination with Poland.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Suspected US strikes pummel Houthi-controlled areas of Yemen
item.description: Suspected U.S. airstrikes have pummeled sites across Yemen controlled by the country’s Houthi rebels, including neighborhoods in the capital, Sanaa.
item.dc:creator: Jon Gambrell, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Australia to hold general elections on May 3 with inflation and a housing shortage major issues
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australians will go to the polls on May 3 for general elections with high costs of living and a shortage of housing likely weighing against the government as Prime Minister Anthony Albanese 's center-left Labor Party seeks a second three-year term.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: World Central Kitchen says Israeli strike killed Gaza volunteer
item.description: The World Central Kitchen (WCK) said that an Israeli military strike killed one of the charity group’s volunteers working in the Gaza Strip and injured six other people. “Our hearts are heavy today as we mourn the loss of one of our volunteers in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Putin says US push for Greenland rooted in history, vows to uphold Russian interest in the Arctic
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russia's President Vladimir Putin said Thursday that President Donald Trump's push for control over Greenland wasn't surprising given longtime U.S. interest in the mineral-rich territory.
item.dc:creator: VLADIMIR ISACHENKOV, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Knife-wielding man injures multiple people in Amsterdam, including 2 Americans
item.description: Dutch police are searching for a motive after five people, including two Americans, were stabbed in a busy shopping area of Amsterdam on Thursday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: King Charles admitted to hospital over side effects from cancer treatment
item.description: The United Kingdom’s King Charles III was admitted to the hospital Thursday due to unexpected side effects from cancer treatments.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: King Charles III briefly hospitalized for observation following side effects from cancer treatment
item.description: Charles' cancer diagnosis has heaped pressure on the British monarchy, which is still evolving after the 70-year reign of the late Queen Elizabeth II.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: An apartheid-era song Elon Musk has decried as anti-white is headline news again in South Africa
item.description: CAPE TOWN, South Africa (AP) — An old anti-apartheid song that was a call for Black people to stand up against oppression and has now been decried by Elon Musk and others as promoting the killing of whites was back in the headlines in South Africa and the subject of an order by its highest
item.dc:creator: GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: US ramps up demands on Ukraine in latest mineral proposal
item.description: President Trump’s administration is ramping up demands on Ukraine in the latest proposal, looking to assume control of all of Kyiv’s critical energy and mineral resources as it pushes ahead to reach a ceasefire in the country’s three-year war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: New 2-clawed dinosaur discovered in Mongolia
item.description: Although the new species has large front claws, it is likely a herbivore who used them to grab leaves.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: Putin predicts Trump will 'systematically' go after Greenland
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin said Thursday the Trump administration likely will move to acquire Greenland “systemically” after months of swipes at the autonomous territory within the Kingdom of Denmark.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: 6 dead after tourist submarine sinks off Egypt's coast: officials
item.description: Six dead, dozens rescued after tourist submarine sinks off the coast on Egypt on Thursday. Officials say all of the victims were Russian. It is not known what caused the sinking.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Human Rights Council blocks watchdog chief from criticizing UN official accused of antisemitism
item.description: The U.N. Human Rights Council blocked Hillel Neuer from finishing his remarks criticizing Special Rapporteur for the Occupied Palestinian Territories Francesca Albanese.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Brazilian President Lula swipes at Trump as US imposes auto tariffs
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva swiped Thursday at U.S. President Donald Trump's decision to raise U.S. tariffs on a wide range of products, saying Trump should consider the potential harm to the American and global economies.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Macron says a proposed European force for Ukraine could 'respond' if attacked by Russia
item.description: PARIS (AP) — French President Emmanuel Macron said Wednesday that a proposed European armed force for possible deployment in Ukraine in tandem with an eventual peace deal could “respond” to a Russian attack if Moscow launched one.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Brazil ex-President Bolsonaro will stand trial over an alleged coup plan. Here’s what happens next
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — A panel of Brazil Supreme Court justices unanimously ruled that former President Jair Bolsonaro and seven of his associates will stand trial on five counts, including attempting to stage a coup after the far-right leader lost the 2022 election.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Signal leak: Trump adviser Mike Waltz may have to go, Warner says
item.description: “There needs to be accountability,” said Sen. Warner, who faults Trump's inner circle with a number of security gaffes.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Palestinians protest Hamas in a rare public show of dissent in Gaza
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Thousands of Palestinians marched between the wreckage of a heavily destroyed town in northern Gaza on Wednesday in the second day of anti-war protests, with many chanting against Hamas in a rare display of public anger against the militant group.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, FATMA KHALED and SARAH EL DEEB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Family unknowingly had rare, valuable painting for 150 years
item.description: A painting hanging in a French family's living room turned out to be far more valuable than just another trinket or heirloom.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel orders more evacuations and Palestinians stage rare anti-Hamas protests
item.description: The Israeli military on Wednesday ordered the evacuation of parts of Gaza City as it steps up its renewed offensive against Hamas after breaking the ceasefire last week.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Brazilian ex-President Bolsonaro ordered to stand trial over alleged coup plan
item.description: Brazil’s Supreme Court accepted charges against ex-President Jair Bolsonaro over an alleged attempt to stay in office after his 2022 election defeat, ordering him to stand trial.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Atlantic releases full Signal text chain
item.description: The Atlantic editor-in-chief on Wednesday released the full text message chain between Trump administration officials.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: iPad submerged 5 years in London's River Thames helps unlock murder plot
item.description: The iPad was covered in dark mud after five years at the bottom of England's longest river, the Thames, but it was the chip inside that helped police crack a five-year-old murder plot.
item.dc:creator: WGN 9

item.title: UN to reduce staff in Gaza, blaming Israel for a strike that killed its employee
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — The United Nations said Monday it will “reduce its footprint” in the Gaza Strip after an Israeli tank strike hit one of its compounds last week, killing one staffer from Bulgaria and wounding five other employees.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, EDITH M. LEDERER and LEE KEATH, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli strikes kill scores in Gaza as Egypt offers new ceasefire proposal
item.description: Israeli strikes across the Gaza Strip killed more than 65 Palestinians over the past day, including women and children, the Gaza Health Ministry said Monday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US, Russian officials meet in Saudi Arabia to talk ceasefire
item.description: Officials hope the talks will allow negotiators to iron out details of a possible limited ceasefire between Russia and Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Pope returns to Vatican after 5-week hospital stay over life-threatening bout of pneumonia
item.description: A weak and frail Pope Francis returned home to the Vatican from the hospital on Sunday after surviving a five-week, life-threatening bout of pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Oleg Gordievsky, Britain's most valuable Cold War spy inside the KGB, dies at 86
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Oleg Gordievsky, a Soviet KGB officer who helped change the course of the Cold War by covertly passing secrets to Britain, has died. He was 86. Gordievsky died March 4 in England, where he had lived since defecting in 1985.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US ranked 24th among happiest countries in the world: Research
item.description: The United States is still one of the most desired countries to live in, but its residents may not be the happiest.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli forces push deeper into Gaza and destroy its only cancer hospital
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israeli forces advanced deeper into the Gaza Strip on Friday and blew up the only specialized cancer hospital in the war-torn territory, as Israeli leaders vowed to capture more land until Hamas releases its remaining hostages.
item.dc:creator: IBRAHIM HAZBOUN, SAM MEDNICK and DAVID RISING, Associated Press, WGN 9
